name: Horava-Lifshitz gravity
summary: Modifies general relativity at short distances by introducing anisotropic scaling between space and time (dynamical critical exponent z=3 in 3+1D), making the theory power-counting renormalisable. The relativistic z=1 limit emerges in the IR, recovering GR at long distances. Effective speed of light, Newton constant, and cosmological constant are all relevant deformations of the deeply nonrelativistic z=3 theory.

addresses_puzzles.note: Lambda emerges as a relevant coupling that flows from the UV. Specific predictions for the present-day Lambda value depend on the RG flow and choice of detailed-balance condition; not derived from first principles.
Anisotropic scaling z=3 in the UV gives spectral dimension d_s = 2 by construction; dimensional reduction is the defining feature of the model.

touches_benchmarks.note: GW propagation in Horava gravity can deviate from c at high frequency; GW170817 constrains the combination of Lifshitz parameters in the IR.
Anisotropic scaling explicitly breaks Lorentz invariance at short distances; SME / GRB photon dispersion bounds constrain how short the violation scale can be.
Relativistic IR limit reproduces GR predictions to high precision; deviations sit at unobservably high frequencies.

introduces.note: Lifshitz scale plus several couplings of higher-derivative spatial-curvature operators.

history: Horava (2009), 'Quantum gravity at a Lifshitz point', Phys. Rev. D 79, 084008, arXiv:0901.3775. Active development through 2010s; the 'projectable' vs 'non-projectable' versions and the question of recovering full diffeomorphism invariance in the IR remain debated. GW170817 has constrained but not closed.

references.citation: Horava, 'Quantum gravity at a Lifshitz point', Phys. Rev. D 79, 084008 (2009)
